Mortgage options

The best loan is the one that fits the complete picture.

Credit, income, cash, property, timeline, and monthly comfort all matter. Compare the paths first; apply when the direction is clear.

Find my likely pathTalk with Dennis

Home purchase

Plan the first home or the next one around the complete move.

The right purchase loan is not automatically the one with the smallest down payment. We compare payment, mortgage insurance, closing costs, reserves, seller credits, assistance, current-home equity, and sale timing so the entire plan works.

First homeMove-up buyerRelocationFHAConventionalFlorida DPA
  • A comfortable payment range, not only the maximum approval
  • A complete estimate of down payment, closing costs, reserves, and sale proceeds
  • Buy-before-sell, contingent-offer, and assistance questions reviewed early
Build a plan for this path →

VA financing

Use the benefit with a veteran in your corner.

Dennis reviews entitlement, Certificate of Eligibility, funding-fee exemptions, residual income, property issues, taxes, insurance, and offer strategy before you are deep in a contract.

0% down for eligible buyersNo monthly PMIIRRRLRepeat useVA jumbo
  • Full or remaining entitlement review
  • Orlando condo and property-condition questions
  • Payment planning with taxes, insurance, and HOA included
Build a plan for this path →

Self-employed & investors

A complex file deserves more than one bank box.

Tax returns do not always tell the whole story. We can compare agency income, bank statements, 1099 income, asset depletion, DSCR, and other specialty paths across multiple wholesale lenders.

Bank statement1099DSCRFix & flipAsset-based
  • Review the document path before a credit pull
  • Compare rate and flexibility, not just approval
  • Keep business and property strategy in the same conversation
Build a plan for this path →

Refinance & home equity

Match the tool to the reason you need the money.

A HELOC, fixed home-equity loan, cash-out refinance, or rate-and-term refinance can produce very different costs and risks. Start with your goal, current first mortgage, timeline, and break-even point.

HELOCHome equity loanCash-out refinanceRate & termVA IRRRL
  • Keep a strong first mortgage in place when that makes sense
  • Compare variable-rate and fixed-payment tradeoffs
  • Calculate break-even before changing the loan
Build a plan for this path →

Your next move

Unsure which section is yours? That is exactly what the plan is for.

Answer a few simple questions and see the likely conversation to have next.
